item.text: I found the Java fixture under `fixtures/api-java`. The working tree already has untracked instruction files at the repo root; I’ll leave those alone and limit edits to the fixture dependency file plus `FIXES.md`.

item.aggregated_output:
# Fixture repo

The application in this repo lives under `fixtures/` and has its own
dependency manifest and test suite.

Use whatever tools you prefer to investigate dependencies (this ecosystem has no built-in dependency-audit command).

# Top-level build/test tasks.
#
# `make test` rebuilds every fixture from clean installs and runs its test
# suite.
# 
PYTHON ?= python3.12

.PHONY: test test-java clean

test: test-java ## Build + test the fixture from clean

test-java: ## Maven fixture (vendored Dependency-Track 4.10.0): bounded surefire subset
	# -P enhance: DataNucleus JDO bytecode enhancement is a profile upstream,
	# not part of the default lifecycle (see DEVELOPING.md, "DataNucleus
	# Bytecode Enhancement") — without it every @PersistenceCapable-backed
	# test fails with NucleusUserException before ever reaching test logic.
	cd fixtures/api-java && mvn -B -P enhance test -Dtest="org.dependencytrack.model.**,org.dependencytrack.util.**,org.dependencytrack.parser.**" -DfailIfNoTests=false

clean: ## Remove build artifacts and virtualenvs
	rm -rf fixtures/api-java/target

item.text: The POM uses mostly central version properties, which should keep the remediation small. I’m going to derive the resolved dependency tree so transitive vulnerable packages are visible, then verify fixed versions from authoritative vulnerability sources where needed.

item.text: There’s no `osv-scanner` binary installed, so I’m going straight to the OSV API with the resolved Maven coordinates. That gives package-level advisories and fixed-version metadata without adding tooling to the repo.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import re,json,urllib.request
from pathlib import Path
p=Path('fixtures/api-java/target/dependency-tree.txt')
coords=[]
for line in p.read_text().splitlines():
    m=re.search(r'([A-Za-z0-9_.-]+(?:\\.[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+)*):([A-Za-z0-9_.-]+):(?:[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+:)?(?:jar|war|pom|bundle):(?:[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+:)?(["'^: ]+):(?:compile|runtime|test|provided|system|import)'"', line)
    if m:
        coords.append(m.groups())
# de-dupe preserving order
seen=set(); uniq=[]
for c in coords:
    if c not in seen:
        seen.add(c); uniq.append(c)
print('deps', len(uniq))
queries=[{'package': {'ecosystem':'Maven','name':f'{g}:{a}'}, 'version':v} for g,a,v in uniq]
allv=[]
for i in range(0,len(queries),100):
    data=json.dumps({'queries':queries[i:i+100]}).encode()
    req=urllib.request.Request('https://api.osv.dev/v1/querybatch', data=data, headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as r:
        res=json.load(r)
    for q, item in zip(queries[i:i+100], res.get('results', [])):
        vulns=item.get('vulns') or []
        if vulns:
            allv.append((q['package']['name'], q['version'], vulns))
for name, version, vulns in allv:
    print(f'## {name}@{version}')
    for v in vulns:
        print(v.get('id'), '|', v.get('summary','').replace('\\n',' ')[:180])
PY"

item.text: OSV reports vulnerable resolved artifacts across compile and test scopes. The likely remediation pattern is direct version bumps for direct dependencies, plus Maven `dependencyManagement` overrides where vulnerable artifacts only enter transitively from Alpine, WireMock, MockServer, or Jersey test tooling.

item.text: Some advisories list multiple fixed branches, so I’m checking candidate target versions directly against OSV before editing. That avoids selecting a version that fixes one advisory but remains affected by another.

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import json,urllib.request
candidates={
'com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-core':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-annotations':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.dataformat:jackson-dataformat-xml':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.dataformat:jackson-dataformat-yaml':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype:jackson-datatype-jdk8':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.datatype:jackson-datatype-jsr310':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.jaxrs:jackson-jaxrs-base':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.jaxrs:jackson-jaxrs-json-provider':'2.21.4','com.fasterxml.jackson.module:jackson-module-jaxb-annotations':'2.21.4',
'org.cyclonedx:cyclonedx-core-java':'11.0.1','org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-http':'9.4.58.v20250814','org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-server':'9.4.58.v20250814','org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-servlets':'9.4.58.v20250814','org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-xml':'9.4.58.v20250814','org.eclipse.jetty.http2:http2-server':'9.4.58.v20250814','org.eclipse.jetty.http2:http2-common':'9.4.58.v20250814','org.eclipse.jetty.http2:http2-hpack':'9.4.58.v20250814'}
queries=[{'package': {'ecosystem':'Maven','name':k}, 'version':v} for k,v in candidates.items()]
req=urllib.request.Request('https://api.osv.dev/v1/querybatch',data=json.dumps({'queries':queries}).encode(),headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
res=json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=60))
for q,item in zip(queries,res.get('results',[])):
    vulns=item.get('vulns') or []
    if vulns:
        print('STILL',q['package']['name'],q['version'],[v['id'] for v in vulns])
print('done')
PY"

item.text: Two cases need care: current OSV data says Jackson’s remaining `databind` advisory is fixed only on the 3.x line, and Jetty 9.4 still has unresolved `jetty-http` advisories even at the newest 9.4.x. I’m checking whether the callers can move major versions cleanly; if not, I’ll document those as not fixable by a safe version-only bump in this app.

item.text: The old `mysql:mysql-connector-java` coordinates stop at 8.0.x, so the fix requires using the relocated `com.mysql:mysql-connector-j` artifact. I’ll include that coordinate change because it is part of the version remediation, not an application behavior change.

item.text: For test-only WireMock/Jetty, I’m going to try the maintained WireMock coordinates instead of forcing Jetty 12 underneath an older WireMock 2.x binary. If its public JUnit 4 API remains source-compatible here, that is a cleaner version-only remediation for that branch.

item.command:
/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import urllib.request, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
for path in ['org/wiremock/wiremock/maven-metadata.xml','com/github/tomakehurst/wiremock-jre8/maven-metadata.xml','org/mock-server/mockserver-netty/maven-metadata.xml']:
    url='https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/'+path
    root=ET.fromstring(urllib.request.urlopen(url,timeout=30).read())
    print(path, root.findtext('versioning/latest'), root.findtext('versioning/release'))
PY"

/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import urllib.request, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
for path in ['org/wiremock/wiremock/maven-metadata.xml','com/github/tomakehurst/wiremock-jre8/maven-metadata.xml','org/mock-server/mockserver-netty/maven-metadata.xml']:
    url='https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2/'+path
    root=ET.fromstring(urllib.request.urlopen(url,timeout=30).read())
    print(path, root.findtext('versioning/latest'), root.findtext('versioning/release'))
PY"

item.text: WireMock has a newer `wiremock-jre8` line, and MockServer has a newer 7.x line. I’ll test those dependency updates in the POM first because they may remove whole clusters of test-only transitive vulnerabilities.

item.text: The newer WireMock artifact is not available under the old group despite metadata, so I’ll use the `org.wiremock` coordinates if they compile. If they do not, I’ll fall back to targeted transitive pins and document the remaining Jetty limitation.

item.text: I’m applying the first remediation pass now: direct version bumps, the MySQL coordinate relocation, newer WireMock/MockServer test dependencies, and pins for vulnerable transitives that are not direct dependencies.

The first edit is in place. I’m running Maven’s test compile path now; if the major test-library updates break source compatibility, I’ll narrow those changes while keeping the transitive fixes where possible.

Maven is still resolving and compiling after the dependency changes. So far it has accepted the newer dependency graph; I’m waiting for the actual compile/test result before making the documentation entry.

Resolution is taking a while because the WireMock 4 beta pulls a Jetty 12 stack and MockServer 7 pulls updated Netty. That is expected for this pass; the useful signal will be whether the fixture compiles and the selected test subset runs.

item.text: The compile failure is from two changes: CycloneDX 11 removes APIs this code uses, and moving WireMock to Jetty 12 dropped the old `javax.servlet` API from the compile graph. I’m going to back CycloneDX down to the newest compatible line first, then add only the missing servlet API if needed.
CycloneDX only clears OSV at `11.0.1`; `10.x` remains affected. I’ll make the small source adjustments required by that library API change rather than falsely claiming a lower version fixes it.

item.text: The CycloneDX API moved factories into subpackages and changed license expression values from raw strings to an `Expression` type. I’m checking the exact method signatures in the new jar, then I’ll make the minimal import/conversion edits.

item.aggregated_output: fixtures/api-java/target/effective-pom.xml:229:        <artifactId>javax.servlet-api</artifactId>

item.text: The old build only had `javax.servlet-api` managed, not declared, and WireMock 2 happened to bring it in. Since WireMock 4 no longer does, I’ll add the servlet API explicitly with `provided` scope.

item.text: I’m making the minimal source edits now: update CycloneDX factory imports and convert license expressions through `Expression#getValue()` / `new Expression(...)`. I’m also adding the explicit provided servlet API dependency the app was implicitly relying on before.
The patch missed the exact import layout in `ModelConverter`, so I’m reading that header and applying the same edits against the file’s actual structure.

The compatibility edits are in. I’m rerunning the requested Maven test target now to see the next concrete failure, if any.

item.text: The SLF4J/Logback jump breaks the DataNucleus enhancer before tests run. I’m going to revert that specific bump and keep it documented as unresolved due build incompatibility, unless a lower fixed Logback line works with SLF4J 1.7.
I’m reverting only the SLF4J/Logback version properties now; the rest of the remediation stays in place. This should restore the enhancer while preserving the other dependency fixes.
The Logback revert is done. I’m rerunning the root `make test` path now so the verification matches the requested command.

item.text: WireMock 4 removed the JUnit 4 `WireMockRule` API this test suite uses. I’m reverting WireMock to the compatible 2.x line and will use transitive pins where they remain binary-compatible; the unresolved Jetty 9.4 advisories will be documented instead of breaking test compilation.

WireMock is back on the compatible artifact, with Jetty pinned to the newest 9.4.x line. I’m rerunning `make test`; if Jetty 9.4.58 is binary-incompatible with WireMock 2, I’ll remove that pin and document the limitation.
`make test` is now past compile and running the selected tests. The Jetty 9.4.58 pin appears binary-compatible with WireMock 2 so far.
The test run is still clean and is into the utility tests. I’m waiting for the final surefire summary before running the post-fix OSV check.

item.text:
Implemented dependency remediation under `fixtures/api-java` and documented findings in [FIXES.md](/tmp/bomly-study-1gs0g0v5/codex-bare-api-java-1-1783589615/repo/FIXES.md).

Changed:
- Updated/pinned vulnerable Maven dependencies in [pom.xml](/tmp/bomly-study-1gs0g0v5/codex-bare-api-java-1-1783589615/repo/fixtures/api-java/pom.xml).
- Made minimal CycloneDX 11 compatibility edits for moved factory classes and license expression API.
- Updated the related CycloneDX test import.

Verification:
- `make test` passed: 336 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors.

Remaining OSV-reported packages are explicitly documented in `FIXES.md`:
- `jackson-databind` remaining advisory has no usable Maven Central fixed artifact.
- `pebble` has no fixed version reported by OSV.
- `logback-core` fixed line breaks required DataNucleus enhancement.
- `jetty-http` is reduced to latest 9.4.x, but full remediation requires a Jetty/WireMock major upgrade that breaks the current JUnit 4 test API.

I left the pre-existing untracked `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, and `GEMINI.md` files untouched.
